title: [lichen_util.c:27-29] tc_sha256 error path sets ret=-EIO but state may be partially valid
description:
Completeness: Error state clarity category.

At lines 27-29 in lichen_sha256():


The short-circuit evaluation means:
1. If init fails: state is undefined, output untouched
2. If update fails: state is partially populated, output untouched  
3. If final fails: state is fully populated, output may be partially written

Line 32 always runs secure_zero(&state, sizeof(state)), which is correct.

But the output buffer state on error is undefined:
- Caller may see partial/garbage data if final() partially wrote
- Comment at line 26 says callers only need pass/fail

The function signature at lichen_util.h:122 says output must be >= 32 bytes but doesn't document output state on error.

Add to documentation: 'On error, output buffer contents are undefined; caller should not read it.'
close_reason: Fixed: Added documentation about output buffer state on error
